The reproducer needs an external membership type to exist before the service accepts it. I’m going to seed that one row directly through the existing test DB helpers, then call `create_type` and `update_type` through `TypeService` so the failure lands on the public contract.

item.text: The Rust guideline file referenced by `guidelines/README.md` is not present in this worktree, so I’m following the local test style already used in this crate. The next edit adds two RED tests: one for create, one for update.

I’m editing only `type_service_test.rs`, reusing its DB seeding and service setup patterns. The test expectations are behavioral: external registered membership codes should round-trip through create/update.
The RED tests are in place. I’m running the exact focused cargo command now and expecting it to fail on the current validation prefix check.

item.text: The focused command is RED for both service paths, and the failures point at the RG-prefix validation applied to external `gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~...` membership codes. I’m collecting the final diff and paths for the artifact.

arguments.summary_title: bug5 verified: resource-group rejects external allowed_membership type codes
arguments.summary_markdown:
Verified RED reproduction for bug5 in `cyberware-resource-group`.

What is broken: `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` reject registered external domain type codes in `allowed_membership_types` because they route each membership code through `validation::validate_type_code`, which requires the resource-group registry prefix `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~`. Membership resource types are allowed to be external domain types, such as `gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~...`.

How reproduced: added two focused service-level tests in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/tests/type_service_test.rs`. Each test seeds a registered external type row in the test database, then calls the public `TypeService` create/update path with that external code in `allowed_membership_types`. The correct behavior is to accept and round-trip the external membership type; the current tree fails before persistence with a validation prefix error.

Evidence: `cargo test -p cyberware-resource-group --test type_service_test -- type_create_accepts_external_allowed_membership_type type_update_accepts_external_allowed_membership_type` exits 101. Both tests fail with `Validation { message: "Type code must start with prefix 'gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~', got: 'gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~...`.

Impacted services/components: `cyberware-resource-group` domain type management, specifically `TypeService` create/update validation and the shared resource-group type code validation helper. `MembershipService::add_membership` / `remove_membership` were not modified.
steps: Add the RED reproduction tests in modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/tests/type_service_test.rs.
Confirm the command fails on the current unfixed tree with validation errors requiring the RG prefix on external membership type codes.
arguments.expected_outcome: Registered external domain type codes such as gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~... are accepted in allowed_membership_types during TypeService::create_type and TypeService::update_type, and they round-trip through get_type.
arguments.actual_outcome: The focused cargo command exits 101. Both tests fail before persistence with Validation errors: `Type code must start with prefix 'gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~', got: 'gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~x.test.member...v1~'` and the same prefix error for the update path.

environment_check.notes: In-memory SQLite test database and crate migrations were used by the existing resource-group test harness.

involved_components.name: cyberware-resource-group::domain::type_service::TypeService
involved_components.reason: create_type and update_type currently validate every allowed_membership_types entry through the RG-prefixed type-code validator, causing external registered membership types to fail.

involved_components.name: cyberware-resource-group::domain::validation
involved_components.reason: validate_type_code enforces the gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~ prefix; it is appropriate for RG type codes but too strict for allowed membership domain type codes.

involved_components.name: cyberware-resource-group::infra::storage::type_repo::TypeRepository
involved_components.reason: The tests seed and resolve registered type rows through the existing type registry storage path, proving the failure occurs before/around service validation rather than because the external type is missing.

content.text: OK: payload validated against the schema and captured. You may end your turn now; no need to repeat the JSON.

item.text:
Artifact submitted. The RED reproducer is in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/tests/type_service_test.rs`, and the regression command fails now as required:

`cargo test -p cyberware-resource-group --test type_service_test -- type_create_accepts_external_allowed_membership_type type_update_accepts_external_allowed_membership_type`

Both failures show `TypeService` rejecting external `gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~...` membership type codes because `validation::validate_type_code` enforces the RG prefix.
